name: Gardens by the Bay
blurb: Iconic Supertree Grove plus two cooled conservatories — the Flower Dome and Cloud Forest with its indoor waterfall. The outdoor gardens are free; only the conservatories are ticketed.
tips: Free Garden Rhapsody light show at the Supertrees daily at 7.45pm and 8.45pm. Buy conservatory tickets online for a small discount. Stroller-friendly throughout.
getting_there: MRT Bayfront (CE1/DT16), Exit B. ~10 min walk via underground link.

name: Singapore Zoo & River Wonders
blurb: Open-concept zoo set in rainforest with elephants, orangutans, and a fragile forest walk-through. River Wonders next door has manatees and giant pandas.
tips: Go at opening (8.30am) before it gets hot. Combo tickets save money if doing both. Tram is included and a lifesaver with tired kids.
getting_there: MRT Khatib (NS14), then free shuttle bus. Or Grab — about 30 min from town.

name: Marina Bay Sands SkyPark
blurb: Observation deck on the 57th floor with sweeping views of the bay, CBD skyline, and Gardens by the Bay from above.
tips: Sunset (around 7pm) is best — you get city, sea, and night lights in one visit. Hotel guests access free; everyone else needs a ticket.
getting_there: MRT Bayfront (CE1/DT16), Exit B or C. Tower 3, follow signage.

name: Hawker Lunch at Maxwell Food Centre
blurb: Classic CBD hawker centre, famous for Tian Tian Hainanese Chicken Rice and a dozen other local legends in one spot.
tips: Go before noon or after 1.30pm to dodge the office crowd. Bring tissue packets — that's how locals 'chope' (reserve) seats.
getting_there: MRT Maxwell (TE17) or Tanjong Pagar (EW15). Right outside the station.

name: Universal Studios Singapore
blurb: Theme park on Sentosa with seven themed zones — Sci-Fi City, Ancient Egypt, Hollywood and more. Mix of thrill rides and family-friendly attractions.
tips: Express Pass is worth it on weekends. Bring a poncho — Jurassic Park Rapids will soak you. Lockers available near wet rides.
getting_there: MRT HarbourFront (NE1/CC29), then Sentosa Express to Waterfront Station.

name: Chinatown Heritage Walk
blurb: Wander Pagoda Street's shophouses, visit Sri Mariamman Temple and the Buddha Tooth Relic Temple, then snack your way through the food street.
tips: Cover shoulders and knees for temple visits. The Heritage Centre on Pagoda Street is a small but excellent museum if you want depth.
getting_there: MRT Chinatown (NE4/DT19), Exit A.

name: Jewel Changi Airport
blurb: Even non-travellers visit for the HSBC Rain Vortex — the world's tallest indoor waterfall — surrounded by a multi-level forest valley.
tips: Canopy Park on level 5 has bouncing nets and mirror mazes — kids love it (separate ticket). Light & sound shows at the vortex hourly from 8pm.
getting_there: MRT Changi Airport (CG2), follow signs to Jewel via T1.

name: MacRitchie TreeTop Walk
blurb: A free-standing suspension bridge in the rainforest, part of a 7km loop trail. Macaques, monitor lizards, and rainforest canopy views.
tips: Start early — gets very humid. Don't carry visible food (monkeys will grab it). Wear proper shoes; trail is uneven in parts. Closed Mondays.
getting_there: Bus 132, 165, 166 to Venus Drive, then 25 min walk to trailhead.

name: Little India & Tekka Centre
blurb: Vibrant streets of Indian shops, garland sellers, and gold jewellers. Tekka Centre downstairs has South Indian breakfasts; upstairs is a colourful wet market.
tips: Go on Sunday for the most lively atmosphere. Don't miss the Sri Veeramakaliamman Temple. Banana leaf rice for lunch is the move.
getting_there: MRT Little India (NE7/DT12), Exit C for Tekka.

name: Sentosa Beaches
blurb: Three beaches — Siloso (busy, beach bars), Palawan (family, Suspension Bridge to a tiny southernmost-point islet), and Tanjong (quiet).
tips: Palawan is best for young kids — calmer water and shaded play area. Free beach shuttle runs the length of the island.
getting_there: MRT HarbourFront, Sentosa Express to Beach Station ($4 entry by Express; free if you walk via boardwalk).

name: Lau Pa Sat Satay Street
blurb: Beautiful Victorian-era hawker centre. After 7pm the adjacent Boon Tat Street closes to traffic and turns into satay heaven with smoky charcoal grills.
tips: Order from any one satay stall — they share tables. Pair with sugarcane juice. Cash speeds things up though most accept QR pay now.
getting_there: MRT Telok Ayer (DT18) or Raffles Place (EW14/NS26).

name: KidZania (now KidZ@Play)
blurb: Mini-city role-play centre where kids try out professions — pilot, firefighter, surgeon — and earn play currency. Best for ages 4–12.
tips: Book a 4-hour slot for the full experience. Adults can sit in the cafe area; you don't follow them around. Bring a water bottle.
getting_there: Currently relocated — check website for latest venue before going.

name: Botanic Gardens & National Orchid Garden
blurb: UNESCO World Heritage site with 160 years of history. The Orchid Garden inside has 1,000+ species. The main gardens are free and stroller-friendly.
tips: Enter via Tanglin Gate for the heritage trees and swan lake. Jacob Ballas Children's Garden inside is excellent and also free.
getting_there: MRT Botanic Gardens (CC19/DT9), Exit A.

name: ArtScience Museum
blurb: Lotus-shaped museum at MBS. The permanent 'Future World' exhibit by teamLab is a digital wonderland — the kids will lose their minds.
tips: Future World gets crowded after lunch — go right at opening (10am). Wear something comfortable; lots of sitting on the floor.
getting_there: MRT Bayfront (CE1/DT16), Exit D.

name: Haw Par Villa
blurb: Eccentric Chinese mythology theme park from the 1930s, free entry. Famous for the gloriously gory '10 Courts of Hell' diorama. Wonderfully weird.
tips: 10 Courts ticketed (small fee) and may scare younger kids — preview first. Mostly outdoors, bring water and a hat.
getting_there: MRT Haw Par Villa (CC25), Exit A.

name: Tiong Bahru Bakery + Estate Walk
blurb: Art deco housing estate (Singapore's oldest) full of indie cafes, bookshops, and the famous bakery's kouign-amann. Great morning stroll.
tips: Bakery queue is fastest before 9am or after 3pm. The wet market hawker centre nearby is excellent for traditional breakfast (chwee kueh, lor mee).
getting_there: MRT Tiong Bahru (EW17), 10 min walk to the estate.

name: Pulau Ubin Day Trip
blurb: Tiny island off the northeast coast — feels like Singapore 50 years ago. Rent bikes and ride to Chek Jawa wetlands.
tips: Bumboat leaves when 12 passengers gather (~$4 each way, cash). Bring insect repellent and water. Half a day is enough; full day if you cycle far.
getting_there: MRT Tanah Merah (EW4), then bus 2 to Changi Village, then bumboat from Changi Point Ferry Terminal.

name: Singapore Discovery Centre
blurb: Hands-on science and history museum out west, with simulators, an outdoor water play area, and a 4D theatre. Underrated and rarely crowded.
tips: Check exhibition rotation before going — keeps things fresh. Cafe options are limited; eat before or at nearby Joo Koon mall.
getting_there: MRT Joo Koon (EW29), then bus 182. Or Grab from town (~30 min).

name: East Coast Park Cycling
blurb: 15km coastal park with rental bikes (including kids' bikes and trailer attachments), beach, BBQ pits, and seafood restaurants.
tips: Rent at Coastline Leisure or GoCycling. Avoid weekend afternoons — gets packed. Try Long Beach Seafood for chilli crab as a reward.
getting_there: MRT Bedok (EW5), then bus 401 (weekends only) or Grab to ECP.

name: Kampong Glam & Haji Lane
blurb: Historic Malay-Arab quarter around the gold-domed Sultan Mosque. Haji Lane next door is narrow and packed with murals, indie shops, and bars.
tips: Visit the mosque outside prayer times; modest dress required. Try Zam Zam for murtabak — a Singapore classic since 1908.
getting_there: MRT Bugis (EW12/DT14), Exit B.

name: National Gallery Singapore
blurb: Southeast Asia's largest visual art museum, set in the gorgeously restored former Supreme Court and City Hall buildings.
tips: Rooftop has free public access with great Padang views. Free entry for Singaporeans/PRs. Family activity guides at the info desk.
getting_there: MRT City Hall (EW13/NS25), Exit B.

name: Mandai Wildlife West (Bird Paradise)
blurb: Replaced the old Jurong Bird Park — eight massive walk-through aviaries with 3,500 birds. Penguins, hornbills, flamingos, lorikeets you can feed.
tips: Go early (opens 9am). Penguin feeding times posted at entrance. Consider the Mandai pass if also visiting Zoo or Night Safari same trip.
getting_there: MRT Springleaf (TE4) or Khatib (NS14), then Mandai Khatib Shuttle.

name: Old Airport Road Food Centre
blurb: Locals' favourite hawker centre with multiple Michelin-mentioned stalls — try Hokkien mee, char kway teow, lor mee, and old-school ice kacang.
tips: Lunchtime 12–1.30pm gets intense. Some legendary stalls close on specific weekdays — check before heading down.
getting_there: MRT Dakota (CC8), Exit B, 5 min walk.

name: Marina Barrage Kite Flying
blurb: Massive grass rooftop overlooking the bay — perfect for kite flying (sea breeze!), picnics, and watching the city light up at dusk.
tips: Buy a cheap kite from a Daiso. Free entry, free parking. Sustainable Singapore Gallery downstairs has good toilets and aircon.
getting_there: MRT Marina Bay (NS27/CE2/TE20), then 15 min walk or short Grab.

name: Singapore Science Centre + Snow City
blurb: Old but consistently good — hands-on physics, a Tesla coil show, butterfly garden. Snow City next door for sub-zero tubing if the kids haven't seen snow.
tips: Allow 3+ hours for Science Centre alone. Snow City supplies jackets and boots — wear long pants and socks.
getting_there: MRT Jurong East (NS1/EW24), 10 min walk.

name: Katong & Joo Chiat Peranakan Trail
blurb: Pastel-coloured Peranakan shophouses on Koon Seng Road — the most photographed street in Singapore. Great laksa nearby at 328 Katong Laksa.
tips: Mornings are best for photos (light + fewer cars). Combine with Rumah Bebe for Peranakan beadwork or Kim Choo for nyonya kueh.
getting_there: MRT Marine Parade (TE26) or Grab from town.

name: Night Safari
blurb: World's first nocturnal zoo. Tram ride through dimly lit habitats with rhinos, hyenas, and Asian elephants. Walking trails branch off.
tips: Last entry 11.15pm but go for the 7.15pm slot to catch the Creatures of the Night show first. No flash photography on the tram.
getting_there: MRT Khatib (NS14), then Mandai Khatib Shuttle.

name: Geylang Serai Market
blurb: Largest Malay market in Singapore. Beautiful at any time, magical during Ramadan with the bazaar. Try mee siam, mee rebus, and kueh.
tips: Wet market downstairs, cooked food upstairs. Joo Chiat Road just south has more eats — try nasi lemak at Boon Lay Power.
getting_there: MRT Paya Lebar (EW8/CC9), Exit C.

name: Fort Canning Park
blurb: Historic hilltop park in the heart of town. The 'Tree Tunnel' spiral staircase is the city's most-photographed spot. Battlebox WW2 bunker tour available.
tips: Tree Tunnel is at Park Connector entrance near River Valley. Battlebox tours need advance booking. Cool morning walk before town gets hot.
getting_there: MRT Fort Canning (DT20) or Dhoby Ghaut (NS24/NE6/CC1).

name: Sungei Buloh Wetland Reserve
blurb: Mangrove wetland in the far north — mudskippers, monitor lizards, kingfishers, and migratory birds (Sept–March). Free, peaceful, totally different vibe.
tips: Bring binoculars if you have them. Boardwalks are stroller-friendly. Wear long sleeves at dusk — mosquitoes. Best in early morning.
getting_there: MRT Kranji (NS7), then Kranji Countryside Express bus or short Grab.

name: Burmese Buddhist Temple (Maha Sasana Ramsi)
blurb: The only Burmese Buddhist temple outside Myanmar built in authentic Burmese style, housing the largest pure white marble Buddha statue outside Myanmar (10 tonnes, 11ft tall). Founded 1875, this site since 1988.
tips: Free entry, daily 6.30am–9pm. Remove shoes before entering. Dress modestly. Weekday mornings are quietest. Sunday Dhamma classes for kids if you want to time a cultural visit.
getting_there: MRT Novena (NS20), Exit B. Walk along Thomson Rd then turn into Tai Gin Rd — about 10 min total. 14 Tai Gin Road.

name: Buddha Tooth Relic Temple
blurb: Tang dynasty-inspired Chinese Buddhist temple in Chinatown, completed 2007, housing what's believed to be a tooth relic of the Buddha. Stunning rooftop orchid garden and a small museum inside.
tips: Free entry. Modest dress required (free shawls available). Don't miss the rooftop garden and giant prayer wheel. Vegetarian canteen on site is cheap and good.
getting_there: MRT Chinatown (NE4/DT19), Exit A. 5 min walk down South Bridge Road.

name: Thian Hock Keng Temple
blurb: Singapore's oldest Hokkien temple (1842), built without nails using traditional southern Chinese architecture. Dedicated to Mazu, goddess of the sea — sailors prayed here before reclamation moved the coastline.
tips: Free entry, daily 7.30am–5pm. The mural along the rear wall depicting early Singapore migrants is exceptional. Le Shan cafe next door for a quiet coffee.
getting_there: MRT Telok Ayer (DT18), 3 min walk. 158 Telok Ayer Street.

name: Kong Meng San Phor Kark See Monastery
blurb: Singapore's largest Buddhist monastery, sprawling complex with multiple halls, pagodas, koi ponds, and a vegetarian restaurant. Surprisingly tranquil despite being in the middle of the city.
tips: Free entry. The Hall of No Form with its 10,000 Buddhas is the highlight. Bright Hill is enormous — allow at least 90 min. Vegetarian buffet on the basement level is excellent and cheap.
getting_there: MRT Bishan (NS17/CC15), then bus 410 (weekends only) or short Grab.

name: Singapore DUCKtours
blurb: Amphibious WWII-style vehicle that drives through Marina Bay area, then splashes into the water for a marine tour. Cheesy quacking sounds, but kids genuinely love it.
tips: Tour is about 60 min. Sit in the front rows for best views. Loud quacker horns are handed out — adjust expectations accordingly. Departs from Suntec City.
getting_there: MRT Promenade (CC4/DT15) or Esplanade (CC3). Tickets at the Suntec City counter.

name: Big Bus / Hop-On Hop-Off Tour
blurb: Open-top double-decker bus loops covering Marina Bay, Chinatown, Little India, Orchard Road, and the Civic District. Good orientation tour for first-time visitors.
tips: 1- and 2-day passes available. Sit on the upper deck but bring sun protection — it gets brutal at midday. Audio commentary via earbuds. Less useful if you already know the city.
getting_there: Multiple boarding stops; Suntec City and Orchard Road are the most convenient starts.

name: Singapore Flyer
blurb: 165m-tall observation wheel — once the world's largest — with views over Marina Bay, the F1 circuit, and on a clear day, parts of Indonesia and Malaysia. One full rotation takes about 30 min.
tips: Sunset slots offer the best views but cost more. Air-conditioned capsules are stroller-friendly. Combo deals with dining inside the capsule available if you're feeling fancy.
getting_there: MRT Promenade (CC4/DT15), Exit A, 5 min walk.

name: Madame Tussauds Singapore
blurb: Wax museum on Sentosa with 80+ figures — Hollywood, K-pop, Bollywood, sports, plus local icons like Lee Kuan Yew. Includes the indoor 'Spirit of Singapore' boat ride and Marvel 4D.
tips: Book online for big discounts (often 30–50% off the gate price). Allow 1.5–2 hours. The Images of Singapore historical section is more substantial than expected — kids may find it draggy.
getting_there: Sentosa Express to Imbiah Station, short walk to Imbiah Lookout.

name: Dopamine Land
blurb: Multi-sensory immersive exhibition with 9 themed rooms — pillow fight arena, neon ball pit, infinity mirror cosmos, dance floor. Equally fun for kids and adults chasing photo ops.
tips: Open Wed–Mon (closed Tuesdays), 11am–9pm. Allow ~45 min to 1.5 hr. Socks required (bring grip socks or buy on-site). Toddlers under 2 free. Combine with Sentosa attractions same day.
getting_there: Sentosa Express to Resorts World Station, walk to WEAVE @ RWS, Level 2.

name: Animal Cafés (Cat / Dog / Reptile)
blurb: Singapore has several themed pet cafés — The Cat Café in Bugis, We Are The Furballs (cats) in Bugis+, and various dog-friendly cafés in Tiong Bahru. Hands-on time with animals plus drinks.
tips: Most charge entry fees + minimum order. Time slots required at popular cafés — book online. Hand sanitiser is mandatory. Better with kids 5+; younger ones can be too rough for the cats.
getting_there: MRT Bugis (EW12/DT14) for the main cluster of cat cafés.

name: Henna Painting in Little India
blurb: Stalls along Buffalo Road and inside Tekka Centre offer henna body art (mehndi) for tourists and locals — intricate floral and paisley designs that last 1–2 weeks.
tips: Prices range $10–40+ depending on size and detail. Negotiate before they start. Designs darken over 24–48 hours. Avoid 'black henna' — real henna is brown/orange-red.
getting_there: MRT Little India (NE7/DT12), Exit C — stalls are immediately around Tekka.

name: Tiong Bahru & Dempsey Café Hopping
blurb: Two of Singapore's best café neighbourhoods. Tiong Bahru: indie bakeries and minimalist coffee in a heritage estate. Dempsey: leafy former British army barracks with brunch institutions like PS Cafe.
tips: Tiong Bahru is walkable; Dempsey needs a Grab between spots. Weekday mornings are blissfully quiet. PS Cafe Harding Road for the iconic forest setting.
getting_there: Tiong Bahru: MRT Tiong Bahru (EW17). Dempsey: Grab from Orchard Road.

name: Southern Ridges Nature Walk
blurb: 10km elevated forest trail connecting Mount Faber, Telok Blangah Hill, Kent Ridge Park, and HortPark. Includes the iconic curved Henderson Waves bridge — Singapore's highest pedestrian bridge.
tips: Start at HarbourFront and exit at Kent Ridge — mostly downhill that direction. Allow 2–3 hours. Sunset on Henderson Waves is unbeatable. Stroller-unfriendly in parts.
getting_there: MRT HarbourFront (NE1/CC29), Exit D, 10 min walk to Marang Trail.

name: Coney Island Park
blurb: Wild, undeveloped 80-hectare island in the northeast connected by bridges. Boardwalks through casuarina forest, beaches, and even a free-roaming Brahman cow population. Feels nothing like Singapore.
tips: Rent bikes from Punggol Point for the best experience. Bring water and repellent. No food stalls inside — eat at Punggol Point Park before/after. Best in cooler morning hours.
getting_there: MRT Punggol (NE17), then LRT to Punggol Point Park, walk across the bridge.

name: Kayaking at Pasir Ris / Changi
blurb: Calm coastal kayaking through mangroves at Pasir Ris Park or along Changi's old beach. Several rental outfits offer guided sunset paddles or self-rental for experienced paddlers.
tips: Kayasia and Asia Detour Kayak Outfitters operate guided trips. Tides matter — check schedule before going. Sunset paddle into the Straits of Johor is magical. Book ahead on weekends.
getting_there: MRT Pasir Ris (EW1) for Pasir Ris Park; Grab to Changi Beach Park.

name: Clarke Quay & Boat Quay Nightlife
blurb: Two adjacent riverside districts of restored shophouses turned bars, clubs, and restaurants. Clarke Quay leans party (Zouk, big clubs); Boat Quay is more laid-back with riverside dining.
tips: Cover charges at clubs but often include 1–2 drinks. Smith Street near Chinatown also has rooftop bars. River taxi between Clarke Quay and Marina Bay is a fun way to bar-hop.
getting_there: MRT Clarke Quay (NE5) or Raffles Place (EW14/NS26) for Boat Quay.
